query: dual-energy CBCT metal artifact reduction brachytherapy applicator imaging
citations: [A comprehensive dual energy method for CBCT metal artifact reduction.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39662056/)
[Feasibility of dual-energy CBCT by spectral filtration of a dual-focus CNT x-ray source.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35113908/)
[Low-cost dual-energy CBCT by spectral filtration of a dual focal spot X-ray source.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38688995/)
[Intra-Guided Dual-Energy Material Decomposition Method for Dual-Source CBCT Systems.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40039907/)
[Feasibility of dual-energy CBCT material decomposition in the human torso with 2D anti-scatter grids and grid-based scatter sampling.](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37477550/)

evidence.claim:
PubMed ID: 39662056. 2024/12/27 00:00. BACKGROUND: Dual-energy (DE) imaging techniques in cone-beam computed tomography 
(CBCT) have potential clinical applications, including material quantification
evidence.source_title: A comprehensive dual energy method for CBCT metal artifact reduction.
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 39662056. 2024/12/27 00:00. BACKGROUND: Dual-energy (DE) imaging techniques in cone-beam computed tomography 
(CBCT) have potential clinical applications, including material quantification 
and improved tissue visualization. However, the performance of DE CBCT is 
limited by the effects of scattered radiation, which restricts its use to small 
object imaging.
PURPOSE: This study investigates the feasibility of DE CBCT material 
decomposition by reducing scatter with a 2D anti-scatter
evidence.search_query: dual-energy CBCT metal artifact reduction brachytherapy applicator imaging
